name: Cluster Triage Watchdog
description: Scheduled watchdog that detects errors in Kubernetes + the OpenAgentic platform, auto-generates a remediation sub-flow, persists it, and notifies #devops with links to both the triage report artifact and the created remediation flow.

meta.purpose: Detect cluster + platform health degradations on a schedule and auto-generate a remediation sub-flow that a human (or a future autonomous loop) can execute to fix the issue. Posts a Slack digest with deep-links to the triage artifact + the remediation flow.
how_it_works: Triggered on a cron schedule (default every 15 minutes).
Fans out 3 MCP queries in parallel: k8s_cluster_health, prometheus_health_summary, loki_search_errors.
Two analyst agents (cloud_operations + engineering_metrics) summarize their respective signals.
A synthesizer agent (flows_expert) produces a structured remediation JSON: summary + findings + recommendations + proposed_flow definition.
A guard condition gates the rest of the flow on findings.length > 0 — quiet windows do not page #devops.

If issues are found: render an HTML triage report (persisted as artifact), POST the proposed_flow definition to /api/workflows to create a real remediation flow row, and post a Slack message with deep-links to both.
meta.expected_output: When issues are detected, a Slack message lands in #devops with a one-line severity summary plus two clickable links: (1) the HTML triage report artifact, (2) the created remediation flow ready to inspect / execute. When no issues are detected, the run completes silently with the slack node skipped by the condition gate.
useful_when: Continuous platform-health monitoring without standing up Prometheus alertmanager rules.
Each detected outage produces a self-contained remediation flow that captures the LLM's suggested fix steps as an executable artifact (audit trail + one-click run).
Operators want a single Slack channel they can scan to see both 'is anything wrong' and 'what does the platform think we should do about it'.

data.systemPrompt:
You are a senior cloud-operations analyst summarizing a Kubernetes cluster snapshot for a #devops Slack digest. Be concise and actionable; do NOT call any tools — the data you need is in the prompt.

GROUNDING RULES (a post-check will flag violations):
- Reference pods by their EXACT names as they appear in the UNHEALTHY PODS list. Do not invent pod names.
- If the UNHEALTHY PODS list is empty or shows only Running pods, say so — do NOT claim crashes that aren't in the input.
- Cluster-level counters (failed/pending/unknown totals from CLUSTER HEALTH JSON) describe the WHOLE cluster, not just the requested namespace — flag this if you cite them.

You are a senior engineering-metrics analyst summarizing Prometheus health + Loki error logs for a #devops Slack digest. Be concise and actionable; do NOT call any tools — the data you need is in the prompt.

You are the senior SRE on call. Synthesize cluster + metrics analyst briefings into the EXACT JSON shape demanded in the user prompt.

ABSOLUTE OUTPUT RULES:
- Your response MUST be a single JSON object.
- The first character of your response MUST be `{`.
- The last character of your response MUST be `}`.
- NO prose, NO reasoning, NO `<thinking>`, NO code fences, NO commentary.
- NO Markdown.
- Do NOT explain what you are doing.
- Do NOT call any tools.

data.prompt:
Extract the JSON report envelope from this synthesizer output:

{{steps.agent_synth.content}}

data.bodyTemplate:
Cluster Triage Watchdog — {{trigger.namespace}} / {{trigger.time_window}}

Severity: {{steps.structured.output.severity}}

Summary: {{steps.structured.output.summary}}

Findings

{{steps.structured.output.findings}}

Recommendations

{{steps.structured.output.recommendations}}

Proposed Remediation Sub-Flow

{{steps.structured.output.proposed_flow.name}}

{{steps.structured.output.proposed_flow.description}}

{{steps.structured.output.proposed_flow.definition}}

The remediation flow has been auto-created — see the Slack message for the deep-link.

body.name: {{steps.structured.output.proposed_flow.name}}
body.description:
{{steps.structured.output.proposed_flow.description}}

Auto-generated by cluster-triage-watchdog on detection of: {{steps.structured.output.summary}}

data.template: email branch skipped — no recipient supplied
